Petition For Approval Of Settlement
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sarah Brooks for and with Scott Wentz.
John Fasesky, Rowena Navia and Justin Henserson for and with Brooke Wentz.
G. Franchesca Callejo, court-appointed attorney for Lydia Seebach Wentz. (via zoom)
Yulissa Zulaica for and with Barbara Simon.

The Court summarized the procedural posture, including the pending probate and civil actions, prior mediation efforts, and the proposed settlement.

Ms. Brooks argued that the proposed settlement was not fair or reasonable, failed to account for additional alleged takings, and improperly affected Scott Wentz's asserted individual claims. She requested an evidentiary hearing on reasonableness, a carve-out preserving her client's individual claims, and that her client does want to settle. She also stated that they never received formal accounting.

Ms. Zulaica argued that Ms. Simon had authority as Trustee and Attorney-in-Fact to resolve the claims and that the settlement was reasonable and consistent with Lydia Wentz's expressed wishes to end the litigation and preserve trust assets. She also stated that the intent is to settle all the claims. Ms. Navia stated that we offered the accounting, we tried to be very clear that Brooke was not the best accountant, and that they wanted the best for mom. Ms. Callejo advised the Court regarding Lydia Wentz's stated wishes, including that she wanted the litigation to end, wanted her estate divided equally, did not wish her daughter punished, and wanted any funds taken to be returned.

Mr. Henderson stated that they invited a counteroffer and has not received one yet.

Mr. Fasesky stated that Brooke has always been financially supported by her mother, and that she never wanted to sue Brooke.

The Court stated that Ms. Zulaica to submit a complete settlement agreement with the promissory note.

Following discussion, the Court shall take this matter under submission. The Court directed the parties to continue settlement discussions and confer regarding the accounting issues.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: A. Datt; Deputy: Not reported (D204).

Petition For Approval Of Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sarah Brooks for and with Scott Wentz.
John Fasesky, Rowena Navia and Annie Nguyen for and with Brooke Wentz.
G. Franchesca Callejo, court-appointed attorney for Lydia Seebach Wentz.
Yulissa Zulaica for and with Barbara Simon.

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Amended First Account and Report and Petition for its Settlement; and 2) Petition for Approval of Settlement.

At the outset, the Court disclosed that it had previously represented Ms. Zulaica's firm years ago. No objections were raised.

The Court summarized the procedural posture, including the pending probate and civil actions, prior mediation efforts, and the proposed settlement.

Ms. Brooks argued that the proposed settlement was not fair or reasonable, failed to account for additional alleged takings, and improperly affected Scott Wentz's asserted individual claims. She requested an evidentiary hearing on reasonableness, a carve-out preserving her client's individual claims, and denial of the petition so the parties could return to the drawing board.

Ms. Zulaica argued that Ms. Simon had authority as Trustee and Attorney-in-Fact to resolve the claims and that the settlement was reasonable and consistent with Lydia Wentz's expressed wishes to end the litigation and preserve trust assets. She requested the opportunity to submit further briefing. Ms. Navia emphasized the cost of continued litigation and its impact on Lydia's resources. Ms. Nguyen opposed the request for an evidentiary hearing and argued in favor of approval of the settlement. Ms. Callejo advised the Court regarding Lydia Wentz's stated wishes, including that she wanted the litigation to end, wanted her estate divided equally, did not wish her daughter punished, and wanted any funds taken to be returned.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to April 28, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Reply briefs were ordered to be filed by April 7, 2026. The Court directed the parties to continue settlement discussions and confer regarding the accounting issues.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

The matter was on calendar for hearing on the amended first account and report and for status of mediation.

The parties reported that mediation had been unsuccessful, and the case remains unresolved. The hearing on status of mediation was taken off calendar. The Court suggested that the accounting petition be continued to a date after the trial currently set for November 3, 2025.

Ms. Brooks requested that the accounting issues also be set for trial for November 3, arguing that the accounting issues are intertwined with the surcharge petition. Other counsel present agreed with the Court's suggestion to continue the accounting petition until after the trial.

Over the objection by Ms. Brooks, the Court declined to set the accounting petition for trial and continued the matter to December 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Mrs. Callejo stated that she met with her client, that Mrs. Wentz' (Lydia) mind is fairly clear on certain issues, that she discussed the issue of a professional fiduciary, and that her client had no opinion on it. Mrs. Callejo also stated that Jordan Goldstein's position is that it is better for her to serve as guardian ad litem and that the other fiduciary Barbara Simon is also willing to serve. Mr. Fasesky stated that the accounting is attached to his declaration. Mr. Fasesky was advised that the accounting needs to be filed as its own document and that he will need to file it as a petition. The parties indicated that Mrs. Simon is acceptable. Mrs. Callejo stated that she will file Barbara Simon's consent to serve. Mrs. Evans asked that the accounting be served on her and verified with all invoices and receipts. The issue of pro bono mediation was raised by Mrs. Callejo. Mr. Wentz expressed concern about the amount of money that has been taken by Brooke, stated that he needs to get some documents first in order to participate in mediation and raised the issue of removing Brooke. Mrs. Callejo suggested that the court appoint of Barbara Simon, remove Brooke Wentz as the successor trustee and the power of attorney, and stated that Brooke and her attorney could file a final accounting. Mrs. Navia stated that she will extend the accounting to the date that Barbara Simon gets appointed. Further discussion was held regarding the family dynamics between Brooke and Scott. Discussion was held regarding the amount of money that has been taken from the trust, the removal of Brooke as power of attorney, the need to have the Mrs. Seebach moved to another room for lack of resources, Mrs. Seebach having signed another power of attorney appointing Scott, the need to proceed with discovery, the parties attempt to file reports regarding Mrs. Seebach's medical condition and capacity under seal, the parties stipulating to said documents being filed under seal, Martin Harris having completed the accounting for the power of attorney as well as the accounting for the trust, the facility no longer acknowledging an agent as there are conflicting powers of attorney, the issue of the court appointing independent counsel to represent Mrs. Seebach, concern about who is going to act as Mrs. Seebach's agent, the changes that have been made to the trust and whether Mrs. Seebach had capacity to execute said changes, money having been transferred to Brooke individually and to two companies that Brooke owns, the court's position that it will not issue any orders today as there are too many disputes, the issue of the court appointing a neutral agent, the issue of mediation, whether it is appropriate to proceed with the move at this time, etc. When asked, Mrs. Seebach stated that she has no complaints, that she would like to stay at her home, that she did not know of any petition against her daughter and that she did not talk to any attorney. The Verified Petition for Elder Financial Abuse, etc. is continued to July 9, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed by June 18, 2024.
